---
title: "Quality in Policy Impact Evaluation"
description: "Detailed study notes for Quality in Policy Impact Evaluation in Magenta Book: Central Government guidance on evaluation."
book_id: "magenta-book"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Appendix"
part: "Appendices and supplementary guidance"
source_pages: "60-73"
citation_key: "hm_treasury_evaluation_task_force_2020_magenta_book"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:magenta-book:quality-policy-impact-evaluation-executive-summary:c0001"
  - "chunk:magenta-book:quality-policy-impact-evaluation-1-introduction:c0001"
  - "chunk:magenta-book:quality-policy-impact-evaluation-1-introduction:c0002"
  - "chunk:magenta-book:quality-policy-impact-evaluation-2-quality-in-policy-impact-evaluation:c0001"
  - "chunk:magenta-book:quality-policy-impact-evaluation-2-quality-in-policy-impact-evaluation:c0002"
source_status: "needs_review"
---

## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2020</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Appendices > Quality in policy impact evaluation</span><span>Source pages 60-73</span><span>Appendix</span></div>

This note summarises Appendices > Quality in policy impact evaluation from HM Treasury and Evaluation Task Force's *Magenta Book: Central Government guidance on evaluation* [@hm_treasury_evaluation_task_force_2020_magenta_book]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked MCP research-library retrieval records, not a substitute for the source text.

The retrieval trail is retained in the source chunks at the bottom of the page. The note paraphrases the guidance and does not reproduce tables, checklists, figures, exhibits, or extended passages. When a checklist or table is central, the note describes its function and leaves the source artifact in the evidence record.

## Core Argument

The policy impact quality guide helps users judge how strongly an impact design can support
attribution and what conclusions are warranted.

## Study Summary

This appendix focuses on empirical impact evaluations that quantify whether observed outcomes were
caused by a policy. It aims to help policy makers and analysts choose designs with clear strengths
and limits.

The central issue is attribution. Monitoring outcomes can show change, but it cannot by itself
establish whether the policy caused that change; stronger designs need a credible counterfactual.

Design quality has to be proportionate to the policy's risk, scale, profile, and learning value. The
guide asks users to consider whether they can afford not to conduct a robust impact evaluation when
causal claims matter.

The guide also recognises feasibility limits. Legal constraints, national rollout, small sample
sizes, weak data, and statutory frameworks may restrict design options, but those constraints do not
change what can validly be concluded.

For close reading, track how this note uses policy impact evaluation, causal attribution,
counterfactual, and internal validity while moving through purpose, impact questions, and quality
and attribution. The notes below treat those moves as study scaffolding: they identify what the
guidance asks the evaluator to notice, what judgment problem it helps solve, and what should be
checked against the saved evidence records before relying on the summary in applied work.

Within the wider Magenta Book route, this appendix supplies specialist depth for the main guidance.
It should be read after the relevant chapter has established the evaluation purpose, questions, data
constraints, and use needs.

## Key Concepts

- **policy impact evaluation**: Impact evaluation applied to government policy, with attention to attribution, design quality, and feasibility.
- **causal attribution**: Reasoning about whether observed outcomes were caused by the intervention rather than other factors.
- **counterfactual**: An estimate of what would have happened without the intervention, used to support impact claims.
- **internal validity**: The credibility of the causal estimate for the intervention and setting being evaluated.
- **external validity**: The extent to which impact findings can inform decisions beyond the specific evaluated setting.
- **robustness**: The extent to which findings remain credible under reasonable checks, assumptions, and alternative analyses.
- **proportionality**: Matching evaluation effort, design, cost, and transparency to risk, scale, uncertainty, and decision value.

## Appendix Structure

- Purpose.
- Impact questions.
- Quality and attribution.
- Design hierarchy.
- Feasibility constraints.
- Interpretation limits.

## Practical Implications

- Match causal claims to the design's ability to construct a credible counterfactual.
- Be explicit when feasibility constraints limit attribution strength.
- Plan impact evaluation early enough to avoid avoidable data and rollout constraints.

## Concept Map

::: {.concept-map}
```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  Problem["Problem: What evaluation problem does Quality in Policy Impact Evaluation help government handle?"]
  Central["Concept: Quality in Policy Impact Evaluation"]
  Concept1["Concept: policy impact evaluation"]
  Concept2["Concept: causal attribution"]
  Concept3["Concept: counterfactual"]
  Mechanism1["Mechanism: purpose"]
  Mechanism2["Mechanism: impact questions"]
  Mechanism3["Mechanism: quality and attribution"]
  Implication1["Policy implication: Match causal claims to the design's ability to construct a credible counterfactual."]
  Implication2["Policy implication: Be explicit when feasibility constraints limit attribution strength."]
  Question["Open question: What evidence would make this guidance credible in a live evaluation?"]

  Problem --> Central
  Central --> Question
  Central --> Concept1
  Central --> Concept2
  Central --> Concept3
  Concept1 --> Mechanism1
  Concept2 --> Mechanism2
  Concept3 --> Mechanism3
  Mechanism1 --> Implication1
  Mechanism2 --> Implication2
  Implication1 --> Question

  classDef problem fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#b91c1c,color:#7f1d1d;
  classDef concept fill:#e6f3f1,stroke:#115e59,color:#134e4a;
  classDef mechanism fill:#e8f1ff,stroke:#1d4ed8,color:#1e3a8a;
  classDef implication fill:#fce7f3,stroke:#be185d,color:#831843;
  class Problem problem;
  class Central concept;
  class Concept1,Concept2,Concept3 concept;
  class Mechanism1,Mechanism2,Mechanism3 mechanism;
  class Implication1,Implication2,Question implication;
```
:::

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Reading route: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Concepts synthesis: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)
- Previous: [Quality in Qualitative Evaluation](appendix-quality-in-qualitative-evaluation.qmd)
- Next: [Realist Evaluation](appendix-realist-evaluation.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What evaluation problem does Quality in Policy Impact Evaluation help diagnose?
2. Which assumptions would need evidence before applying this guidance in a live evaluation?
3. How would this change the way an evaluator scopes questions, methods, reporting, or use?
4. Where could the guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## Source Chunks

<details class="source-chunks">
<summary>Source chunks</summary>
<ul class="source-chunk-list">
<li><code>chunk:magenta-book:quality-policy-impact-evaluation-executive-summary:c0001</code></li>
<li><code>chunk:magenta-book:quality-policy-impact-evaluation-1-introduction:c0001</code></li>
<li><code>chunk:magenta-book:quality-policy-impact-evaluation-1-introduction:c0002</code></li>
<li><code>chunk:magenta-book:quality-policy-impact-evaluation-2-quality-in-policy-impact-evaluation:c0001</code></li>
<li><code>chunk:magenta-book:quality-policy-impact-evaluation-2-quality-in-policy-impact-evaluation:c0002</code></li>
</ul>
</details>

## References

- HM Treasury and Evaluation Task Force. (2020). *Magenta Book: Central Government guidance on evaluation*. HM Treasury. Appendices > Quality in policy impact evaluation, pages 60-73.
- Saved retrieval metadata: `data/research-library/evidence/magenta-book/chapter-evidence-summary.json`.
